title_zh: 通过主机卸载减少基于JAX的大型语言模型训练中的高带宽内存瓶颈
title_en: Reducing High-Bandwidth Memory Bottlenecks in JAX-Based LLM Training with Host Offloading
summary_zh: 大型语言模型(LLM)训练任务经常在GPU计算资源未完全利用时遇到显存限制。模型权重、梯度和优化器状态等数据占用大量显存，限制了训练规模。
summary_en: Large language model (LLM) training workloads often hit GPU memory limits before fully utilizing compute resources. Model weights, gradients, and optimizer states consume significant GPU memory, constraining training scale.
why_it_matters_zh: 通过将部分数据卸载到主机内存，可以有效缓解GPU显存压力，提高训练效率，支持更大规模模型的训练。
why_it_matters_en: Offloading parts of the data to host memory effectively alleviates GPU memory pressure, enhances training efficiency, and enables training of larger-scale models.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: Reducing High-Bandwidth Memory Bottlenecks in JAX-Based LLM Training with Host Offloading. Category: coding_ai. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.

title_zh: NVIDIA CUDA中的内核融合：优化内存传输与启动开销
title_en: Kernel Fusion in NVIDIA CUDA: Optimizing Memory Traffic and Launch Overhead
summary_zh: 本文介绍了如何通过内核融合技术提升GPU代码性能，具体包括提高内存带宽利用率和减少内核启动的开销。
summary_en: This post explains how kernel fusion can enhance GPU code performance by improving memory bandwidth utilization and reducing kernel launch overhead.
why_it_matters_zh: 内核融合通过将多个内核合并，减少内存访问次数和调度延迟，从而显著提升GPU计算效率。
why_it_matters_en: Kernel fusion merges multiple kernels to reduce memory accesses and scheduling delays, significantly boosting GPU computational efficiency.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: Kernel Fusion in NVIDIA CUDA: Optimizing Memory Traffic and Launch Overhead. Category: coding_ai. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.

title_zh: AI模型协同设计：硬件友好的大型语言模型设计
title_en: AI Model Co-Design: Hardware-Friendly LLM Design
summary_zh: AI性能取决于三个维度：准确性（模型的推理和输出质量）、吞吐量（每秒处理的令牌数）和延迟（响应时间）。NVIDIA开发博客介绍了如何通过模型与硬件的协同设计来提升大型语言模型的整体性能。
summary_en: AI performance hinges on three dimensions: accuracy (the model's reasoning and output quality), throughput (tokens processed per second), and latency (response time). The NVIDIA Developer Blog discusses enhancing large language model performance through co-design of model and hardware.
why_it_matters_zh: 了解硬件和模型协同优化能够帮助开发更高效、更强大的AI系统，是推动AI技术进步的重要方向。
why_it_matters_en: Understanding the co-optimization of hardware and models aids in developing more efficient and powerful AI systems, driving advancement in AI technology.

summary_zh: 利用如OpenFold3等模型的生物分子结构预测与共折叠现已成为驱动药物发现和蛋白质研究的大规模主流工作负载。
summary_en: Biomolecular structure prediction and co-folding using models such as OpenFold3 have become mainstream, large-scale workloads that drive drug discovery and protein research.

title_zh: 揭示公众舆论：基于LSTM与传统模型的情感分析研究
title_en: Unveiling Public Opinion: A Study of Sentiment Analysis Using LSTM and Traditional Models
summary_zh: 本文研究了使用LSTM和传统机器学习模型对推特文本进行情感分析的方法。通过对数据集进行分词、词形还原和去停用词预处理，比较了逻辑回归、随机森林、朴素贝叶斯、梯度提升及LSTM等算法的效果。结果显示，LSTM模型在训练准确率90.98%、测试准确率80.00%及ROC-AUC 0.92的表现上优于传统方法，体现了其在捕捉文本上下文和序列特征方面的优势。
summary_en: This paper studies sentiment analysis on Twitter data using LSTM and various traditional machine learning models. After preprocessing steps including tokenization, lemmatization, and stopword removal, models such as logistic regression, random forest, naive bayes, gradient boosting, and LSTM were evaluated. The LSTM model outperformed others with a training accuracy of 90.98%, testing accuracy of 80.00%, and a micro-average ROC-AUC score of 0.92, highlighting its superior ability to capture contextual and sequential text features.
why_it_matters_zh: 随着社交媒体上海量用户生成内容的涌现，准确理解公众情感变得尤为重要。LSTM在情感分析中的出色表现帮助提升了舆论解读及趋势预测的准确性，推动相关领域的发展。
why_it_matters_en: As massive user-generated content floods social media, accurately understanding public sentiment is crucial. The LSTM model’s superior performance enhances interpretation of public opinion and trend forecasting, advancing applications in this domain.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: Unveiling Public Opinion: A Study of Sentiment Analysis Using LSTM and Traditional Models. Category: coding_ai. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.

title_zh: 从求解器到研究：大型语言模型驱动的形式数学研究前沿
title_en: From Solvers to Research: Large Language Model-Driven Formal Mathematics at the Research Frontier
summary_zh: 近期在数学AI（AI4Math）领域，特别是基于大型语言模型（LLM）的定理证明器，在利用交互式定理证明语言生成形式化证明方面取得显著进展。但现有系统在处理前沿数学研究，如发现新定理或解决悬而未决的猜想时仍受限，因其问题多为开放式、多层次抽象。本文综述该领域，包括数据集、自自动形式化和证明合成，指出现有系统作为数学研究代理的核心瓶颈，并提出未来AI4Math的发展路线图。
summary_en: Recent advances in AI for Mathematics (AI4Math), particularly Large Language Model (LLM)-based theorem provers, have achieved notable success in formal proof generation using Interactive Theorem Proving (ITP) languages. However, current systems are fundamentally limited in tackling cutting-edge mathematical research challenges such as discovering new theorems or resolving open conjectures, which are often open-ended and abstract. This position paper systematically reviews the field, covering datasets, auto-formalization, and proof synthesis. It identifies key limitations of existing systems as research agents and outlines a strategic roadmap for the future of AI4Math.
why_it_matters_zh: 此研究强调从预定义求解器向具有严谨形式推理能力的数学研究代理转变，推动AI在前沿数学研究中发挥更大作用，促进自动化数学发现。
why_it_matters_en: The study emphasizes a shift from predefined problem solvers to mathematical research agents capable of rigorous formal reasoning, enabling AI to contribute more effectively to frontier mathematical research and automated discovery.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: From Solvers to Research: Large Language Model-Driven Formal Mathematics at the Research Frontier. Category: coding_ai. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.
ranking_reason: Selected from authoritative primary source; quality score 70.

title_zh: DeepSearch-World：可验证环境中的深度搜索代理自我蒸馏
title_en: DeepSearch-World: Self-Distillation for Deep Search Agents in a Verifiable Environment
summary_zh: 本文介绍了DeepSearch-Evolve，一种基于DeepSearch-World可验证环境的自我蒸馏框架，用于训练网页搜索代理。DeepSearch-World包含42万个多跳问答任务，支持进度验证、反思和故障恢复等关键认知行为，有助于代理自我进化。DeepSearch-Evolve通过轨迹生成、筛选、数据混合和微调，逐步提升代理性能。无需从更强模型蒸馏，DeepSearch-World-9B在BrowseComp、GAIA和HotpotQA等测试集上表现优异，展示了可验证环境对于长远网页代理自我进化的潜力。本文还将开放环境、数据集、模型和代码，促进相关研究。
summary_en: This paper presents DeepSearch-Evolve, a self-distillation framework built on the DeepSearch-World, a deterministic and verifiable environment for training web search agents. DeepSearch-World includes 420K multi-hop QA tasks and supports key cognitive capabilities such as progress verification, grounded reflection, and failure recovery to enable agent self-evolution. DeepSearch-Evolve iteratively generates, filters, mixes data, and fine-tunes agents to improve performance. Without distillation from stronger models, DeepSearch-World-9B achieves competitive results on BrowseComp, GAIA, and HotpotQA benchmarks, demonstrating the value of verifiable environments in scalable self-improvement of long-horizon web agents. The environment, datasets, model, and code will be released to support future research.
why_it_matters_zh: 该研究通过构建一个可验证的训练环境及海量多跳问答任务，突破了以往工具使用代理难以从自身经验中持续进化的瓶颈，实现了一种无需依赖外部更强模型的自我蒸馏方法，推动了长期网页搜索代理自我改进的可扩展性。
why_it_matters_en: This work overcomes the challenge of training tool-use agents that can improve from their own experiences by creating a verifiable environment with large-scale multi-hop QA tasks. It introduces a self-distillation approach that does not require stronger external teacher models, thus advancing scalable self-improvement for long-horizon web agents.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: DeepSearch-World: Self-Distillation for Deep Search Agents in a Verifiable Environment. Category: coding_ai. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.
ranking_reason: Selected from authoritative primary source; quality score 79.

title_zh: 通过幂变换与保符号评分聚合及自适应特征保留实现大型语言模型的结构化剪枝
title_en: Structured Pruning of Large Language Models via Power Transformation and Sign-Preserving Score Aggregation with Adaptive Feature Retention
summary_zh: 本文提出了一种改进的大型语言模型结构化剪枝方法，解决了将无结构剪枝技术自适应特征保留（AFR）应用于结构化剪枝时的三大难题：异构剪枝评分的分布不匹配、方向一致性指示的符号信息丢失及异常值影响。该方法结合了幂变换实现非线性分布对齐、保符号的评分聚合和基于百分位的异常值去除。实验证明，在Llama-3-8B、Vicuna-v1.5-13B和LLaVA-v1.5-13B模型上，该方法保持了与无结构剪枝相当的准确度，同时通过结构化剪枝实现了实际推理加速。
summary_en: This paper proposes an improved structured pruning method for large language models (LLMs) that tackles key challenges when adapting Adaptive Feature Retention (AFR), an unstructured pruning technique, to structured pruning. Specifically, it addresses distribution mismatch between heterogeneous pruning scores, loss of sign information indicating consistent optimization direction, and the influence of outliers. The unified approach combines power transformation for nonlinear distribution alignment, sign-preserving score aggregation, and percentile-based outlier removal. Experiments on Llama-3-8B, Vicuna-v1.5-13B, and LLaVA-v1.5-13B show that the method maintains accuracy comparable to unstructured pruning while achieving practical inference speedup via structured pruning.
why_it_matters_zh: 结构化剪枝相较无结构剪枝在加速推理和部署效率上具有优势，但直接应用无结构剪枝技术存在多重挑战。本文创新性地通过幂变换、符号保留评分聚合及异常值处理策略有效解决这些问题，推动了大型语言模型的高效压缩与加速。
why_it_matters_en: Structured pruning offers practical inference speedup and deployment benefits over unstructured pruning but adapting existing unstructured pruning methods encounters challenges such as score distribution mismatch and loss of directional sign information. This work innovatively integrates power transformation, sign-preserving aggregation, and outlier removal to overcome these obstacles, contributing to more efficient compression and acceleration of large language models.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: Structured Pruning of Large Language Models via Power Transformation and Sign-Preserving Score Aggregation with Adaptive Feature Retention. Category: research_papers. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.
ranking_reason: Selected from authoritative primary source; quality score 65.

title_zh: 利用低成本无人机和起重机摄影测量技术进行落叶树三维重建以监测整冠的枝条伸长
title_en: 3D Reconstruction of Deciduous Trees Using Low-Cost UAV- and Crane-Based Photogrammetry for Monitoring Shoot Elongation Across Entire Canopies
summary_zh: 树木生长决定了大气中二氧化碳的固定量及其在木质生物量中的暂时储存，同时受气候变化带来的高温、干旱、晚霜等极端事件影响。虽然径向生长的连续监测已有成熟的树径仪技术，枝条伸长（初级生长）的监测因缺乏合适测量手段而被忽视，使得气候变化对初级生长的影响了解不足。本研究基于原生落叶树的三维重建，利用低成本无人机摄影测量和多摄像机起重机系统，实现对整冠枝条伸长的测量与监控。实验在两个区域进行，评估了传感器、数据采集及处理策略。结果表明，使用小于250g的消费者级无人机可实现5-6毫米的三维点准确度，重建完整度达92%-98%。论文还引入了3D打印的地面真值枝条用于验证细节重建能力，并讨论了操作挑战及基于点云的树木骨架化初步尝试。
summary_en: Tree growth determines CO2 sequestration and woody biomass storage, influenced by climate change factors such as temperature rise, drought, and frost. While radial growth is continuously measured using dendrometers, shoot elongation monitoring is largely underdeveloped due to lack of suitable methods, limiting understanding of climate change effects on primary growth. This work reconstructs native deciduous trees in 3D using low-cost UAV photogrammetry and a multi-camera CraneCam system over an entire growing season in two study areas. Sensor evaluations, data acquisition, and photogrammetric processing strategies are presented. Results show 3D point accuracies of 5-6 mm using consumer-grade UAVs under 250 g and reconstruction completeness between 92% and 98%. A novel 3D-printed ground-truth branch assesses fine-detail reconstruction capabilities. Challenges and initial skeletonization experiments based on photogrammetric point clouds are discussed.
why_it_matters_zh: 该研究填补了对初级树木生长监测技术的缺口，提供了一种低成本且高精度的三维重建方法，可用于全面监测树冠内枝条的生长变化，对理解和应对气候变化影响具有重要意义。
why_it_matters_en: This research addresses the lack of monitoring methods for primary tree growth by providing a low-cost, high-accuracy 3D reconstruction approach to comprehensively monitor shoot elongation across entire canopies, which is crucial for understanding and responding to climate change impacts.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: 3D Reconstruction of Deciduous Trees Using Low-Cost UAV- and Crane-Based Photogrammetry for Monitoring Shoot Elongation Across Entire Canopies. Category: image_ai. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.

title_zh: 我怎样知道下一句该说什么？巴伦霍尔茨的自生理论作为哈里斯整合主义的丰富补充
title_en: How Do I Know What to Say Next? Barenholtz's Autogenerative Theory as an Enrichment of Harrisean Integrationism
summary_zh: 罗伊·哈里斯的整合语言学批判了传统的指称主义方法，认为语言是一种面向未来共同行动的情境性双向活动，而非映射预先存在世界的代码。但该理论对符号维持开放性的结构机制、语言与非语言符号活动的连续性及过往整合积累的结构特性解释不足。本文提出埃兰·巴伦霍尔茨的自生语言理论，可以填补这些空白，丰富整合主义而不违背其核心观点，提供了开放性的结构机制、越界符号活动的计算对应及存档理论，对自然语言处理和大语言模型设计提供了理论支持。
summary_en: Roy Harris's Integrationist linguistics challenges traditional referentialist views by framing language as a situated, bipartite activity aimed at joint future action rather than a code for a pre-given world. However, it leaves gaps regarding the structural mechanism sustaining prospective openness, the continuity between linguistic and non-linguistic semiotic activity, and the nature of the accumulated archive of past integrations. This paper argues that Elan Barenholtz's autogenerative theory addresses these gaps, enriching Integrationism without undermining it. It offers a structural mechanism for openness, a computational correlate for semiotic continuity, and a theory of the archive. This synthesis aids NLP practitioners and LLM designers in understanding the statistical structures LLMs exploit and their inherent limitations.
why_it_matters_zh: 该研究通过引入巴伦霍尔茨的自生理论，补充了哈里斯整合主义在语言开放性机制和符号活动连续性方面的不足，深化了理论对大语言模型行为的解释，帮助AI研究者更好理解语言模型的统计结构及其局限。
why_it_matters_en: By integrating Barenholtz's autogenerative theory, the study fills explanatory gaps in Harris's Integrationism related to openness and continuity of sign usage. It deepens theoretical understanding of LLM behavior and offers AI researchers a principled account of the statistical structures these models utilize and their inherent constraints.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: How Do I Know What to Say Next? Barenholtz's Autogenerative Theory as an Enrichment of Harrisean Integrationism. Category: coding_ai. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.
ranking_reason: Selected from authoritative primary source; quality score 58.

title_zh: 通过人类与大型语言模型协作构建可扩展且具有文化特异性的刻板印象数据集
title_en: Scalable and Culturally Specific Stereotype Dataset Construction via Human-LLM Collaboration
summary_zh: 研究大型语言模型中的刻板印象多集中于英语环境，因其他语言缺乏数据集且手工标注成本高。本文提出一种高效的人类与大型语言模型协作标注框架，构建了EspanStereo，这是一套涵盖欧洲和拉美多西班牙语国家的刻板印象数据集。EspanStereo包含已知刻板印象及独特文化偏见，通过大型语言模型生成候选刻板印象，由本土标注者验证，体现框架有效识别细微地区偏见。该数据集评估显示不同国家的西班牙语支持模型存在显著刻板行为差异，强调文化基础评估的必要性。该框架适用于其它语言及地区，为多语言刻板印象基准测试提供扩展途径。
summary_en: Research on stereotypes in large language models (LLMs) has primarily focused on English contexts due to a lack of datasets and high annotation costs in other languages. This work introduces a cost-efficient human-LLM collaborative annotation framework to build EspanStereo, a Spanish-language stereotype dataset spanning multiple Spanish-speaking countries in Europe and Latin America. EspanStereo captures both documented stereotypes and culturally specific biases absent from English-centric resources. Using LLMs to generate candidate stereotypes and in-culture annotators for validation, the framework effectively identifies nuanced, region-specific biases. Evaluations of Spanish-supporting LLMs reveal significant variation in stereotypical behaviors across countries, highlighting the need for culturally grounded assessments. The framework is adaptable beyond Spanish, enabling scalable multilingual stereotype benchmarks.
why_it_matters_zh: 该研究填补了多语言刻板印象数据缺乏的空白，提出的人机协作标注方法降低了成本，提升了刻板印象识别的文化针对性，有助于推动更全面、多元的偏见分析和评估。
why_it_matters_en: This work addresses the lack of stereotype datasets in non-English languages by proposing a human-LLM collaboration framework that reduces annotation costs and enhances cultural specificity in bias identification, facilitating more comprehensive and diverse stereotype analysis and evaluation.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: Scalable and Culturally Specific Stereotype Dataset Construction via Human-LLM Collaboration. Category: coding_ai. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.
ranking_reason: Selected from authoritative primary source; quality score 80.

title_zh: 去偏见反而适得其反：基于预处理的刻板印象缓解方法的反直觉副作用
title_en: When Debiasing Backfires: Counterintuitive Side Effects of Preprocessing-Based Stereotype Mitigation
summary_zh: 基于预处理的刻板印象缓解方法，如在去偏见语料上进行训练，虽能减少目标群体的可测刻板印象，但研究发现它们常引发意外副作用，即对其他人口群体的刻板印象或反刻板印象相较中性基线增加，这种效应跨模型类型、预处理策略和训练数据规模均存在。传统基准测试往往未能察觉这些变化。通过注意力展开分析，副作用与注意力流变化不大，增加了解释难度。作者讨论了评估影响，提供可操作诊断，并呼吁重视副作用、推进透明的缓解实践。
summary_en: Preprocessing-based stereotype mitigation methods, such as training on debiased corpora, reduce measurable stereotypes for targeted groups but often cause unintended side effects where stereotyping or counter-stereotyping increases for other demographics, even unrelated ones. These effects are demonstrated across encoder-only and decoder-only models, multiple preprocessing approaches, and varied training scales on Wikipedia. Standard benchmarks frequently fail to detect these shifts. Attention-rollout analysis shows minimal changes in attention flow, complicating mechanistic understanding. The paper discusses evaluation implications, offers actionable diagnostics, and advocates for side-effect-aware and transparent mitigation practices.
why_it_matters_zh: 该研究揭示了当前常用的去偏见预处理方法可能在无意中加剧其他群体的刻板印象风险，挑战了去偏见技术的效果评估和应用安全性，强调了全面监控和透明实践的重要性。
why_it_matters_en: This work exposes that commonly used preprocessing debiasing methods can unintentionally increase stereotypes for non-target groups, challenging the reliability and safety of debiasing techniques and highlighting the necessity for comprehensive monitoring and transparent mitigation strategies.

title_zh: 基于MiniLM嵌入的多簇边界学习方法用于超范围意图检测
title_en: A Multi-cluster Boundary Learning Method for Out-of-Scope Intent Detection via MiniLM Embedding
summary_zh: 本文针对意图检测中的超范围（OOS）意图识别提出了一种基于MiniLM（all-MiniLM-L6-v2）嵌入的多簇边界学习方法。该方法将OOS检测视为单类分类问题，通过学习训练语句的多簇嵌入边界，实现对域外语句的准确拒绝。实验证明，在CLINC150、StackOverflow和Banking77数据集上，该方法的OOS检测表现优于多种基线模型，且MiniLM嵌入更适应该工作流程与语句特征。代码随附公开。
summary_en: This work addresses out-of-scope (OOS) intent detection by proposing a multi-cluster boundary learning method using MiniLM (all-MiniLM-L6-v2) embeddings within a one-class classification framework. The method learns boundaries of multi-cluster embeddings generated from training utterances to reject out-of-domain utterances as OOS intents. Experiments on CLINC150, StackOverflow, and Banking77 datasets show state-of-the-art OOS detection performance compared to baselines. Ablation studies confirm MiniLM's suitability for this workflow and utterance embedding requirements. The code is publicly available.
why_it_matters_zh: 传统多分类方法在意图类别多时准确率下降，且LLM嵌入方法参数庞大难以部署。该方法通过多簇边界学习有效解决了这些问题，实现了更准确、更轻量级的OOS意图检测，适合实际应用场景。
why_it_matters_en: Traditional multi-class classification suffers accuracy drop as intent classes increase, and LLM embeddings require large parameters making deployment challenging. This method effectively addresses these issues by multi-cluster boundary learning, achieving more accurate and lightweight OOS intent detection suitable for practical use.

title_zh: 当不合理的词元被强化：用于大语言模型强化学习的尾部感知信用校准
title_en: When Implausible Tokens Get Reinforced: Tail-Aware Credit Calibration for LLM Reinforcement Learning
summary_zh: 强化学习显著提升了大语言模型的推理能力，但常用的无审稿者强化学习方法均匀分配信用，导致罕见且错误的尾部词元获得与合理词元相同的正面信用，称为正信用污染问题。本文提出尾部感知信用校准（TACO）方法，计算每个词元的尾部风险分数，调整其正面信用，从而降低噪声影响并保留有用罕见模式。实验证明TACO在三个大语言模型和八个基准上优于GRPO基线，提升了训练稳定性和长期强化学习表现。源码公开于Github。
summary_en: Reinforcement learning has enhanced large language models' reasoning, but critic-free RL methods assign uniform credit to all tokens, causing low-probability erroneous tail tokens to get the same positive credit as plausible ones—a problem termed Positive-Credit Contamination. The paper proposes Tail-Aware Credit calibration (TACO), which computes a tail-risk score based on local context to adjust positive credit for risky tokens, suppressing noise while preserving useful rare patterns. Experiments on three LLMs and eight benchmarks show TACO outperforms GRPO baselines, improves training stability, and supports sustained long-horizon RL gains. Source code is available on GitHub.
why_it_matters_zh: 本文识别并解决了强化学习中普遍存在的正信用污染现象，提出通过尾部风险评分精细校准正面信用的创新方法，有效减少了错误词元被强化的问题，提升了模型训练的稳定性和性能，在推动大语言模型强化学习技术进步方面具有重要意义。
why_it_matters_en: The work addresses a critical flaw in reinforcement learning credit assignment known as Positive-Credit Contamination, introducing an innovative tail-risk scoring mechanism to finely tune positive credit. This reduces reinforcement of erroneous tokens, enhances training stability, and boosts performance, making a significant contribution to advancing large language model RL methods.
